content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES CorviMissloN
P.O. Box 807
428 J Street
• Sacramento, CA 958 12-0807
(916)322-5660
• Fax(916)322-0886
October 27, 1998
William N. Sauer, Jr.
Law Offices of William N. Sauer, Jr.
120 Birmingham Drive, Suite 250
Cardiff-by-the-Sea, CA 92007
Re:
Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. 98-263
Dear Mr. Sauer:
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on
October 22, 1998, by the Fair Political Practices Commission.
The Political Reform Act (the “Act”)’ requires the Commission to provide formal written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person’s authorized
representative. (Section 83114 (b).) However, 2 Cal. Code Regulations Section 18329 (b)(8)(A)
provides that written advice will be declined where the “requestor is seeking advice relating to
past conduct.” Because your letter seeks advice regarding a decision already participated in by
Mr. Mitchell, we are unable to provide assistance with respect to the issues you raise in your
letter.
If you need assistance with respect to a specific prospective situatio:, you should request
further assistance. However, we will need detailed facts regarding the situation to which the
request relates.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sinprely,
Lisa L. Ditora
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
LLD:tls
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

sections.conclusion: Because your letter seeks advice regarding a decision already participated in by Mr. Mitchell, we are unable to provide assistance with respect to the issues you raise in your letter.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice regarding a decision that had already been participated in by Mr. Mitchell, relating to compliance with the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the requestor was seeking advice relating to past conduct, which is prohibited under California Code Regulations Section 18329(b)(8)(A). The FPPC indicated it could assist with prospective situations if detailed facts are provided.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The advice sought related to past conduct, which triggered the regulatory bar to providing assistance. Requestor was invited to submit new requests for prospective situations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought advice regarding a decision that had already been participated in by Mr. Mitchell, relating to compliance with the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Because your letter seeks advice regarding a decision already participated in by Mr. Mitchell, we are unable to provide assistance with respect to the issues you raise in your letter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide formal written advice to William N. Sauer, Jr. regarding past conduct involving Mr. Mitchell's participation in a decision, citing regulatory restrictions on advice relating to past conduct.
